# @aganitha/atk-debug

Thin wrapper around the `debug` package. Adds `[file:line]` caller metadata to every log and runtime production guards. Full API parity with `debug` — anything that works with `debug` works here.

## Install

```
bun add @aganitha/atk-debug
npm install @aganitha/atk-debug
```

## Import

```ts
import debug from '@aganitha/atk-debug';
```

## Create a logger

```ts
const log = debug('service:module');
log('started');                        // service:module [file.ts:5] started +0ms
log('request %s %d', 'GET', 200);
log('data: %O', { key: 'value' });    // %O uses util.inspect
```

## Enable namespaces

Programmatic (must call before logging):
```ts
debug.enable('app:*');
debug.enable('app:*,-app:verbose');   // negation with -
```

Via environment variable (preferred for flexibility):
```
DEBUG=app:* bun run dev
DEBUG=app:*,-app:verbose bun run dev
```

IMPORTANT: `devOnly` defaults to `true` — when `NODE_ENV === 'production'`, `enable()` is a no-op and all logs are suppressed. Call `debug.setDevOnly(false)` first if you need logs in production or in test environments that set `NODE_ENV=production`.

## Extend namespaces

```ts
const base  = debug('service:auth');
const login = base.extend('login');   // → 'service:auth:login'
const db    = login.extend('db');     // → 'service:auth:login:db'
```

## Format strings (inherited from debug)

| Token | Renders as |
|-------|------------|
| `%s`  | String coercion |
| `%d`  | Number |
| `%i`  | Integer |
| `%f`  | Float |
| `%O`  | `util.inspect` (pretty, multiline) |
| `%o`  | `util.inspect` (compact) |
| `%j`  | `JSON.stringify` (built-in, overridable) |

Pass objects as separate arguments — never concatenate strings with objects:
```ts
log('user %O', user);        // correct
log('user ' + user);         // wrong — loses structure
```

## Runtime controls

### devOnly — production suppression (default: true)

```ts
debug.setDevOnly(true);    // suppress when NODE_ENV === 'production'
debug.setDevOnly(false);   // disable production guard
debug.getDevOnly();        // → boolean
```

### callerMeta — [file:line] prefix (default: true)

```ts
debug.setCallerMetaEnabled(false);  // remove [file:line] prefix (performance)
debug.setCallerMetaEnabled(true);   // re-enable
debug.getCallerMetaEnabled();       // → boolean
```

Disabling caller metadata reduces overhead for high-frequency logging paths.

## Enable / disable at runtime

```ts
debug.enable('ns:*');           // enable; respects devOnly
const prev = debug.disable();   // disable all; returns previous namespace string
debug.enable(prev);             // restore
debug.enabled('ns:module');     // → boolean (respects devOnly)
```

## Conditional logging — skip expensive work

```ts
const log = debug('app:trace');
if (log.enabled) {
  log('expensive result: %O', buildReport());
}
```

## Redirect output — per logger

```ts
import fs from 'fs';
const log = debug('app:jobs');
log.log = (...args) => fs.appendFileSync('app.log', args.join(' ') + '\n');
```

## Redirect output — global (all loggers)

```ts
const original = debug.log;
debug.log = (...args) => myStream.write(args.join(' ') + '\n');
// restore:
debug.log = original;
```

## Custom formatters

```ts
debug.formatters.h = (v) => Buffer.from(String(v)).toString('hex');
log('hex: %h', 'hello');    // → hex: 68656c6c6f

debug.formatters.j = (v) => JSON.stringify(v);
log('json: %j', obj);
```

## inspectOpts — control util.inspect depth

```ts
debug.inspectOpts = { depth: 2, colors: false };
```

## Logger instance properties

```ts
log.namespace   // → 'service:module'
log.enabled     // → boolean (reflects devOnly + enable state)
log.color       // → color assigned to this namespace
log.diff        // → ms elapsed since last call on this logger
log.destroy()   // → boolean, unregisters the logger
```

## Static properties / methods

```ts
debug.formatters        // Record<string, (v: unknown) => string>
debug.names             // string[] — active enabled patterns
debug.skips             // string[] — active negated patterns
debug.colors            // (string | number)[] — color palette
debug.humanize(1500)    // → '2s'
debug.selectColor(ns)   // → color value for namespace
debug.coerce(value)     // → debug-printable value
debug.useColors()       // → boolean
debug.save(namespaces)  // persist to storage
debug.load()            // → string | undefined, restore from storage
```

## Type exports

```ts
import type { AtkDebug, DebugWithMeta, DebugTools } from '@aganitha/atk-debug';
```

- `AtkDebug` — type of the default export (callable + all static methods)
- `DebugWithMeta` — type of an individual logger (`debug('ns')` return value)
- `DebugTools` — type of the static methods surface

## Key behaviors

- **devOnly is on by default.** Tests or non-Node environments that do not set `NODE_ENV` to something other than `'production'` will log normally. Only `NODE_ENV === 'production'` suppresses.
- **Caller metadata resolves the first non-library stack frame.** Works correctly through wrapper functions and `extend()`.
- **`enable()` under devOnly=true + NODE_ENV=production is a silent no-op.** Set `devOnly(false)` before `enable()` when you need programmatic control regardless of environment.
- **All loggers share global `devOnly` and `callerMeta` flags** — changing them affects every logger in the process.
- **`extend()` propagates caller metadata correctly** — the child logger reports the actual callsite, not the extend() declaration.

## Common patterns

```ts
// Hierarchy by service layer
const svc  = debug('payments:service');
const repo = debug('payments:repo');
const http = debug('payments:http');

// Namespace-per-file, extend for sub-operations
const log  = debug('auth:session');
const read = log.extend('read');
const write = log.extend('write');

// Structured object logging
log('event %s payload %O', eventName, { userId, action, ts: Date.now() });

// Performance guard
debug.setCallerMetaEnabled(false);
for (const item of hugeList) {
  log('processing %d', item.id);
}
debug.setCallerMetaEnabled(true);
```
